name: SkLearn Lasso Regression
description: Lasso Regression is a linear regression technique that applies L1 regularization to shrink coefficients and perform feature selection by driving some coefficients exactly to zero.

fields.name: targetCol
fields.title: Target Column
fields.description: Target column containing the continuous response variable that the Lasso regression model learns to predict.

fields.name: featureCols
fields.title: Feature Columns
fields.description: List of input feature columns used for training the model. All features must be numeric, boolean, or vector-compatible.

fields.name: splitRatio
fields.title: Split Ratio
fields.description: Ratio used to split the dataset into training and testing sets. A value of 1.0 uses the entire dataset for training.

fields.name: alpha
fields.title: Alpha
fields.description: Regularization strength controlling the amount of L1 penalty applied to coefficients. Higher values increase sparsity by shrinking more coefficients to zero.

fields.name: fit_intercept
fields.title: Fit Intercept
fields.description: Determines whether an intercept term is included in the regression model. Disabling this forces the model to pass through the origin.

fields.name: normalize
fields.title: Normalize
fields.description: If enabled, input features are normalized before regression. This option is ignored when Fit Intercept is set to False.

fields.name: precompute
fields.title: Precompute
fields.description: Specifies whether to use a precomputed Gram matrix to speed up optimization. Useful for datasets with many samples but fewer features.

fields.name: max_iter
fields.title: Max Iterations
fields.description: Maximum number of optimization iterations allowed for the coordinate descent solver to converge.

fields.name: tol
fields.title: Tolerance
fields.description: Convergence threshold for optimization. Training stops when coefficient updates fall below this value and the duality gap is sufficiently small.

fields.name: warm_start
fields.title: Warm Start
fields.description: When enabled, reuses coefficients from a previous fit as initialization, allowing incremental training or faster convergence.

fields.name: positive
fields.title: Positive
fields.description: Forces all learned coefficients to be non-negative, which can be useful when domain constraints require positive relationships.

fields.name: random_state
fields.title: Random State
fields.description: Seed controlling randomness when using randomized feature updates, ensuring reproducible training behavior.

fields.name: selection
fields.title: Selection
fields.description: Defines how coefficients are updated during optimization. Cyclic updates features sequentially, while random selects features randomly to potentially speed up convergence.
